content: The sky darkened suddenly, a strange crackle in the air. I grabbed my kids, pulling them away just as a jagged bolt ripped through the tree nearby. Then, a searing pain exploded through me—I’d been struck. I crumpled, heart hammering, but somehow conscious. When I woke, everything was... different. Sounds were sharper: the rustle of a squirrel’s tail, the whisper of distant voices. Colors blazed with newfound intensity—the green of leaves, the blue of the sky, even the faintest hues I’d never noticed before. Smells flooded me: the damp earth, blooming flowers, even the faint scent of a stranger’s cologne yards away. I doubted it at first—overhyped stories, right? But no, this was real. My senses had amplified a hundredfold, revealing a world I’d always walked through blindly. Now, every moment is vivid, every detail impossible to ignore. This lightning strike didn’t just change me—it awakened me.
